Effective Date of RepealSection 101 of S. 2939, 97th Congress, 2d Session, as reported , and incorporated by reference in , to be effective as if enacted into law, provided that the repeal is effective .Sept. 22, 1982Oct. 1, 1982section 101(e) of Pub. L. 97–276

Reports Covering Fiscal Year Ending September 30, 1982Section 101 of S. 2939, 97th Congress, 2d Session, as reported , and incorporated by reference in , to be effective as if enacted into law, provided in part that the reports required by subsec. (e) of this section with respect to the fiscal year ending , be filed notwithstanding the repeal. Subsec. (e) of this section had required that within thirty days following the end of each fiscal year, the Secretary of the Senate and the Sergeant at Arms and Doorkeeper file reports with the Senate Committee on Appropriations detailing the use and implementation of the authority contained in this section and that such reports include the names of all employees receiving merit compensation under authority of this section at the end of the fiscal year, the positions occupied by them and the date when each such employee first began to receive merit compensation.Sept. 22, 1982Sept. 30, 1982section 101(e) of Pub.
